pr.title: Match some inline asm in ftcommon and HSD aobj
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Replaced several inline-assembly implementations with C in fighter common code and HSD AObj loading. The PR converted ftcommon functions func_8007DD7C, func_8007DFD0, and func_8007E2A4, plus HSD_AObjLoadDesc, while adding a confirmed Fighter Vec2 field at offset 0x2C4 and updating the func_8007E2A4 prototype to return HSD_GObj*. The evidence is almost entirely from the diff; there was no PR body or review discussion beyond approval.
what_changed: Removed large inline-assembly blocks in src/melee/ft/ftcommon.c for func_8007DD7C, func_8007DFD0, and func_8007E2A4 and replaced them with C implementations.
Added a local inline f32 fabsf(f32 x) helper in ftcommon.c to reproduce the absolute-value compare pattern used by the matched code.
Added Fighter field /* 0x2C4 */ Vec2 x2C4 by shrinking filler_x294 from ending at 0x2CC to ending at 0x2C4.
Updated func_8007E2A4 from an untyped/void declaration to HSD_GObj* func_8007E2A4(HSD_GObj*) in ftcommon.h and removed the stale declaration from include/functions.h.
Converted HSD_AObjLoadDesc in src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c from inline assembly to C.
Removed associated #pragma peephole on / push scaffolding around the former inline asm regions.
smart_moves: Promoted the repeated raw offset 0x2C4 access in Fighter into a typed Vec2 field, letting the C code express the original x/y range checks directly as fp->x2C4 and cur_ft->x2C4.

Preserved matching-sensitive local temporaries and stack-padding arrays such as unused2, unused, temp_r31, phi_r28, and sp24/sp1C in the C replacements, indicating the match depended on stack layout and register allocation.

Expressed func_8007E2A4 as a small semantic wrapper around CollData ground normal fields, making the shield knockback vector calculation clearer while also correcting the function signature to return the input HSD_GObj*.

Used existing setter/helper functions in HSD_AObjLoadDesc, including HSD_AObjSetFlags, HSD_AObjSetRewindFrame, HSD_AObjSetEndFrame, HSD_FObjLoadDesc, HSD_AObjSetFObj, HSD_IDGetDataFromTable, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, and HSD_JObjUnref, instead of directly preserving all asm-side field stores.

Kept the explicit ref_count increment and __assert("object.h", 0x5D, "HSD_OBJ(o)->ref_count != HSD_OBJ_NOREF") behavior in HSD_AObjLoadDesc, preserving an important object-lifetime check from the original asm.
decomp_lessons: When replacing asm with C, first promote raw offsets into struct fields if the same offset is central to the function; here Fighter+0x2C4 became Vec2 x2C4 and made the ftcommon match tractable.
Do not assume a function is void just because old global prototypes are incomplete; func_8007E2A4 matched as HSD_GObj* returning its argument.
Small helper functions can be preferable to library calls for matching: the inline fabsf helper produced the needed compare/negate behavior without relying on external libm behavior.
For complex control-flow matches, retaining decompiler-style temporary names and artificial unused stack arrays can be a practical intermediate step.

Baselib object loaders may need to preserve both high-level setter calls and manual reference-count semantics; HSD_AObjLoadDesc still explicitly increments HSD_Obj ref_count and asserts against HSD_OBJ_NOREF.
naming_conventions: Fighter fields follow offset-prefixed names such as x2C4, x98_atk_shield_kb, xF4_ground_attacker_shield_kb_vel, xF8_playerNudgeVel, x6F0_collData, x221F_flag, and x1A58_interactedFighter.
Temporary variables in matched C retained decompiler/register-oriented names such as temp_r31, temp_r0, temp_r30, temp_f0, temp_f1, phi_r28, vtmp, and tmp.
HSD object code uses HSD_* type and function prefixes: HSD_AObj, HSD_AObjDesc, HSD_FObjDesc, HSD_Obj, HSD_JObj, HSD_AObjLoadDesc, HSD_AObjAlloc, HSD_FObjLoadDesc, HSD_JObjUnref.
Boolean state in fighter flags is accessed through bitfield names like cur_ft->x221F_flag.bits.b3 and cur_ft->x221F_flag.bits.b4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced asm nofralloc functions with C while preserving stack-sensitive dummy local arrays, suggesting stack-frame shape was important for the match.
Used a custom inline fabsf implemented as an if/else returning -x or x to mirror the original fcmp/fneg/fmr pattern.
Used ternary expressions for sign-dependent velocity additions, matching repeated branches that choose positive or negative p_ftCommonData values.
Kept apparently redundant assignments and variables in HSD_AObjLoadDesc, such as phi_r30 and a duplicated hsd_obj != NULL check, likely to preserve control flow/codegen.
Converted direct offset operations in func_8007E2A4 into field expressions over CollData normal.x/normal.y and Fighter velocity fields while maintaining the original negation order.

review_feedback.note: No issue comments, review comments, or PR body text were present in the slice, so there is no detailed human rationale or requested-change feedback to extract.

searchable_terms: Match some inline asm in ftcommon and HSD aobj
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Fighter::x2C4 or document what the Vec2 represents.
Search for later cleanup of func_8007DD7C and func_8007DFD0 temporary names and unused stack arrays.
Check whether HSD_AObjLoadDesc received additional type cleanup around aobjdesc->obj_id and HSD_Obj/HSD_JObj casts.
Find callers of func_8007E2A4 to confirm that the HSD_GObj* return value is used or purely for matching/callback compatibility.
